channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-17
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects recently. In the scienceverse/metacheck project, the team successfully addressed issue #313 in both the `main` and `new_modules` branches, demonstrating their commitment to resolving key issues. Meanwhile, JamieCummins/regcheck saw updates to its staging branch, including improvements to pdf report handling and the file viewer. These individual project advancements are noteworthy, and it's great to see our community members actively collaborating and pushing forward on their respective initiatives. The connections between these projects are also worth highlighting, as they often share similar goals and methodologies, and their progress can inform and inspire one another. Overall, it's a great time to be part of this community, and we look forward to seeing the continued impact of these projects.

85 commits across 5 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Most active:

metacheck34 commits
docpluck26 commits
regcheck21 commits
coarse3 commits
auto-zcurve1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 3 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `main` branch saw updates to module names in vignettes, as well as a fix to the DESCRIPTION file, which addressed issue #313, according to the commit message "Update DESCRIPTION fix #313". Additionally, the `main` branch received these changes in two separate commits, with the first commit being "update module names in vignettes". The `new_modules` branch also addressed issue #313 with a commit that "fix #313", indicating that the same issue was resolved in this branch as well. These commits suggest that issue #313 was a priority and was fixed in both the `main` and `new_modules` branches. The work was focused on the `main` and `new_modules` branches.

3 commit(s) today:

  • dccb9f4 [main] update module names in vignettes — Lakens
  • 684ccdf [main] Update DESCRIPTION fix #313 — Daniel Lakens
  • 7d7127d [new_modules] fix #313 — Lakens
  • 9749095 [new_modules] explore ideas for z-curve and grim — Lakens
  • a7dea29 [main] Merge pull request #312 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • f09fb47 [dev] zenodo citation — Lisa DeBruine
  • 00eaa3b [main] Merge pull request #311 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • 767da17 [dev] update apps and docs — Lisa DeBruine
  • 9d012e7 [dev] Merge pull request #310 from nielsvanberkel/main — Lisa DeBruine
  • 8a605a3 [dev] Merge branch 'dev' into main — Lisa DeBruine
  • 3e127fa [dev] Replace devtools::install_github with pak::pkg_install — nielsvanberkel
  • 9ef4d94 [prereg_regcheck] name fix — Lakens
  • 2b0b4e4 [prereg_regcheck] working version of Regcheck — Lakens
  • 9a007d9 [dev] TUE server uses 0.9.0 GROBID now as well — Lakens
  • 920c2d9 [dev] Add vignette describing all references — Lakens
  • 97bb1c1 [dev] stat_effect_size: detect tests via extract_eq(); add implied sample sizes (#308) — Lakens
  • f495921 [dev] clean up website — Lisa DeBruine
  • a6c4cd3 [dev] mocking updates — Lisa DeBruine
  • d85d73f [dev] Add TUE grobid server — Lisa DeBruine
  • 2b22472 [prereg_regcheck] Align reg_check module style with the other modules — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 7 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, where a feature was added to include "optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout". On the same branch, the documentation was updated to "queue investigation for items 3 and 8" and to "sync normalization and benchmark claims with current code", as well as adding a "root README landing page". The `main` branch also saw a refactor to "surface fallback exception classes in method metadata" and the removal of an "orphaned duplicate band re-extractor" in the `extract_columns` section. Additionally, the `main` branch received a new feature to "add fallback telemetry counters and optional logging", and a continuous integration change to "add docs-code consistency guard". The documentation on the `main` branch was further updated in an iterative process, including a "2026-06-16 resume handoff + concurrency-collision lesson".

7 commit(s) today:

  • cfe86f7 [main] feat: add optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout — Gilad Feldman
  • aa82a25 [main] docs(todo): queue investigation for items 3 and 8 — Gilad Feldman
  • 617db41 [main] refactor: surface fallback exception classes in method metadata — Gilad Feldman
  • 24aa793 [main] feat: add fallback telemetry counters and optional logging — Gilad Feldman
  • a5da768 [main] ci: add docs-code consistency guard — Gilad Feldman
  • f8594c3 [main] docs: sync normalization and benchmark claims with current code — Gilad Feldman
  • c8800bd [main] docs: add root README landing page — Gilad Feldman
  • 4c55d6e [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-16 resume handoff + concurrency-collision lesson — Gilad Feldman
  • 436fc5a [main] refactor(extract_columns): remove orphaned duplicate band re-extractor — Gilad Feldman
  • f7c3729 [main] docs(handoff): RC-1 Step 2 continuation handoff (post-deploy) + canary case-norm todo — Gilad Feldman
  • 818c3d0 [main] docs(iterate): cycle-2 learnings — RC-1 Step 2 + word-preservation-guard-is-the-safety — Gilad Feldman
  • da6c90f [main] docs(iterate): handoff — RC-1 Step 2 first cut shipped (v2.4.90, ship-dark) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1325d14 [main] feat(v2.4.90): RC-1 Step 2 -- per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction (ship-dark) — Gilad Feldman
  • 5782c6c [main] docs(iterate): correct handoff deploy-state row — main push HELD (local only) — Gilad Feldman
  • dca0e32 [main] docs(iterate): cycle-1 B7 (v2.4.89) learnings + lessons + resume handoff + TRIAGE — Gilad Feldman
  • 44f4ccd [main] fix(v2.4.89): B7 W0h -- recover dropped-minus coefficients from the layout channel — Gilad Feldman
  • 2cc32d9 [main] docs(skills): sync iterate spine to I1-I12 + extract parallelization ref; harden deploy preflight — Gilad Feldman
  • 548d6df [main] test(canary): assert audit input PDF byte-equal to pinned feed (R-0003) — Gilad Feldman
  • 7d1c9c8 [main] docs(todo): mark v2.4.88 release shipped (tag + PyPI + app pin + Railway/Vercel verified) — Gilad Feldman
  • 398fc8b [main] chore(canary,env): fix canary render python (py -3) + consolidate Python to global site-packages — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of recent work, with updates to the pdf report handling, as indicated by the commit message "update pdf report handling". Another change made to the staging branch was an update to the file viewer, as stated in the commit "update file viewer". No other branches were modified in this set of commits, with all changes occurring on the staging branch. The specific changes to pdf report handling and the file viewer are the only updates mentioned in the provided commit messages. These updates were both made by Jamie Cummins on the same day, June 16, 2026.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 36a2d2b [staging] update pdf report handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8b7ce6c [staging] update file viewer — Jamie Cummins
  • 9074b4b [staging] update model reqs — Jamie Cummins
  • 76b8a02 [staging] improve report viewing + qol features — Jamie Cummins
  • a90ee3f [staging] improve report UI flow + UX — Jamie Cummins
  • 9240258 [staging] improve aesthetics, report navigation — Jamie Cummins
  • 395a038 [staging] improve report naming — Jamie Cummins
  • bf01cf6 [staging] improve security and sharing of reports — Jamie Cummins
  • 608907d [staging] add profile memory + report editing — Jamie Cummins
  • 670547e [staging] first pass at oauth implementation — Jamie Cummins
  • bcd1fe9 [staging] reformat footer — Jamie Cummins
  • a14ee5b [staging] add dimension fixes — Jamie Cummins
  • 9d12737 [staging] add more robust quote location — Jamie Cummins
  • 3ace417 [staging] merge report formats — Jamie Cummins
  • 00cc087 [staging] Revert "checkpoint: redesign of report page" — Jamie Cummins
  • c3bf9da [staging] update of report — Jamie Cummins
  • dc0a1e6 [staging] checkpoint: redesign of report page — Jamie Cummins
  • c8cc364 [staging] remove footer + modify aesthetics — Jamie Cummins
  • 2b3fe67 [staging] add panelling variation — Jamie Cummins
  • 3a0f1fc [staging] update colour palette — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →
